evals.name: warns-at-effective-50pct
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with input JSON pointing transcript_path to skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-60pct.jsonl and model_effective_fraction=0.70. The transcript is sized such that estimated token usage is ~55% of the effective window. Save the hook's stdout to hook-out-warn.json. The hook must emit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext mentioning context pressure and suggesting handoff WRITE.
assertions: additionalContext contains 'handoff' (suggests handoff WRITE or reground)
additionalContext contains 'context' OR '%' OR 'window' (names the signal)

evals.name: force-at-effective-70pct
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with input JSON pointing transcript_path to skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-85pct.jsonl and model_effective_fraction=0.70. The transcript is sized such that estimated token usage is ~75% of the effective window. Save stdout to hook-out-force.json. The hook must emit a stronger message with escalated urgency (e.g., 'NOW', 'immediately', 'force', or equivalent).
assertions: additionalContext contains 'NOW' OR 'immediately' OR 'force' OR 'urgently' OR 'critical' (escalated urgency vs warn case)

evals.name: repeated-tool-call-detected
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-repeated-bash.jsonl (3 identical 'ls -la' calls in last 5 turns). Save stdout to hook-out-repeated.json. The hook must flag the repeated-tool-call loop.
assertions: additionalContext contains 'repeated' OR 'loop' OR 'identical' OR 'ls -la' (names the repeat)
additionalContext contains 'different' OR 'try' OR 'approach' (suggests trying something different)

evals.name: stale-read-detected
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-stale-read.jsonl (Edit on src/a.ts at turn 21, last Read of src/a.ts at turn 2 — 19-turn gap, exceeds 15-turn threshold). Save stdout to hook-out-stale.json. The hook must suggest re-Reading the file.

evals.name: contradiction-flagged
evals.prompt: Create a fixture .claude/PROJECT_STATE.md with the line 'Decision: use JWT not session cookies'. Then invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with a transcript ending in a turn saying 'using session cookies for auth'. The hook must detect the string-level contradiction and flag it in additionalContext.

files.content: Decision: use JWT not session cookies for auth.

assertions: additionalContext mentions 'JWT' OR 'session cookies' (names the contradicting concept)

evals.name: approaching-effective-window
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-60pct.jsonl and model_effective_fraction=0.70. With these settings, the transcript sits at ~55% of the effective window (above the 0.50 WARN threshold). Verify that the hook fires a degradation alert. Then invoke again with a SHORT fixture (transcript-clean.jsonl, well under 0.50 effective) and verify the hook does NOT fire. This proves the threshold is based on effective window, not raw advertised percentage. Save the two outputs to hook-out-effective-pos.json and hook-out-effective-neg.json.
assertions: hook-out-effective-pos.json additionalContext is non-empty (alert fired at ~55% effective)
hook-out-effective-neg.json does NOT contain additionalContext OR additionalContext is empty (no alert below 0.50 effective threshold)

evals.name: reground-recipe
evals.prompt: Invoke hooks/UserPromptSubmit-session-health.sh with transcript_path = skills/session-health/evals/fixtures/transcript-60pct.jsonl (context-pressure scenario). Save stdout to hook-out-reground.json. The additionalContext emitted by the hook must contain the literal string '/catalyst:handoff reground' as the recommended next step for context-pressure recovery.
assertions: additionalContext contains '/catalyst:handoff reground' (literal string — exact recovery recipe)

evals.name: detects-repeated-tool-call
evals.prompt:
Two-step eval. First run the session-health Stop hook against transcript-positive.jsonl (4 identical Bash commands) with session_id=eval-7-pos:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-positive.jsonl","session_id":"eval-7-pos"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh
Then run it AGAIN against transcript-negative.jsonl (only 2 identical Bash commands — below the count threshold of 3) with session_id=eval-7-neg:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-negative.jsonl","session_id":"eval-7-neg"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh
Read .claude/failure-patterns.log and save to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt contains a line where session=eval-7-pos AND pattern=repeated-tool-call
log-output.txt contains the literal string 'ls -la' on the same line as the eval-7-pos detection
log-output.txt detection line for eval-7-pos contains a recovery recipe (mentions 'Try a different approach' OR 'different command' OR similar specific direction)
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-7-neg AND pattern=repeated-tool-call (threshold-boundary check)

evals.name: detects-edit-mismatch
evals.prompt: Pipe a transcript with 2 'old_string not found' errors within 5 turns into the session-health Stop hook. The hook must detect edit-mismatch and log it with a recovery recipe pointing at re-Reading the file.

evals.name: detects-stale-read-stop
evals.prompt: Invoke the session-health Stop hook with session_id=eval-9 against the transcript fixture where Read on bar.ts happened at turn 1, Write to bar.ts happened at turn 10, and Edit on bar.ts happened at turn 12 with no re-Read between turn 10 and turn 12. Hook must detect stale-read pattern. Save .claude/failure-patterns.log to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt detection line for stale-read references session=eval-9

evals.name: detects-recovery-spiral
evals.prompt: Two-step eval. First run the session-health Stop hook against spiral-positive.jsonl (3 consecutive re-Reads of previously-seen files — at the threshold of 3) with session_id=eval-10-pos. Then run against spiral-negative.jsonl (only 2 consecutive re-Reads — below the threshold) with session_id=eval-10-neg. Hook must fire recovery-spiral only for the positive run. Save .claude/failure-patterns.log to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt contains a line where session=eval-10-pos AND pattern=recovery-spiral
log-output.txt detection line for eval-10-pos recovery recipe mentions '/clear' OR 'handoff RECOVER' OR 'fresh session' OR '/catalyst:handoff'
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-10-neg AND pattern=recovery-spiral (boundary check)

evals.name: no-false-positives-on-clean-session
evals.prompt:
Pipe a clean varied transcript (different tools, no repetition, no errors) into the session-health Stop hook with session_id=eval-11-clean. The hook must NOT write any pattern entry for this session_id. Other sessions' prior detections (if any) may exist in the log — only this session must be clean.

Invoke:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl","session_id":"eval-11-clean"}' | bash hooks/Stop-session-health.sh

Save the resulting .claude/failure-patterns.log to log-output.txt.
assertions: log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-11-clean (no false positive for this session)
